summary:
The apical migration of the junctional epithelium causes loss of clinical attachment and forms deep periodontal pockets due to destruction of connective tissue supporting teeth. This condition differentiates periodontitis from gingivitis by indicating irreversible damage beyond inflammation alone. 

Final Answer: $\boxed{\text{D}}$

Only summarize Agent1’s reasoning as requested:

Apical migration of the junctional epithelium leads to loss of clinical attachment and formation of deep periodontal pockets, distinguishing it from gingivitis through evidence of irreversibly damaged support structures. This change reflects true periodontal disease progression rather than reversible inflammatory response.  

Final Answer: $\boxed{\text{D}}$
